arts.ttl: Bayern Munich Reacts to PSG’s Bold Offer for Arsenal, Inter Milan Target as Key Details Emerge
arts.sum: Paris Saint-Germain have been closely monitoring Bayern Munich midfielder Joshua Kimmich for months, nearly signing him last summer. Now, they are once again pushing and have reportedly made an offer. Journalist Christian Falk’s latest report on Kimmich’s future reveals that the veteran has received a solid offer from PSG. The Parisians are likely looking to […] The post Bayern Munich Reacts to PSG’s Bold Offer for Arsenal, Inter Milan Target as Key Details Emerge appeared first on PSG Talk.

arts.ttl: PSG's post-Mbappe era, no 'clans' and foreign stars' French buy-in
arts.sum: Liverpool face a Paris St-Germain side in the Champions League who are hitting their best form of the post-Kylian Mbappe era.

arts.ttl: Paris Saint-Germain v Liverpool: Champions League last 16, first leg – live
arts.sum: Live Champions League updates from 8pm UK time Why not email Scott with your thoughts So much for coming top of the league stage, then. Liverpool’s reward is a tie with Paris Saint-Germain, who have won their last ten games straight, scoring at a rate of exactly four goals per game. In the last month, the champions of France have put four goals past fellow Champions League outfits Monaco and Lille, and seven past Champions League surprise package Brest. Ousmane Dembélé is finally delivering on all that early promise – he’s scored 20 goals in his last 14 appearances – while Bradley Barcola isn’t that backwards in coming forwards either, with 15 goals to his name this season. After all those wasted galáctico years, they’re finally looking like a proper team. Just ask Manchester City. Having said all that, Liverpool, with 30-goal Mohamed Salah in outrageous form himself, are pretty handy too. So given all that, plus the fact these clubs are historically neck and neck with two wins apiece – all four matches ending in home wins, PSG pipping the 1997 Cup Winners Cup semi, Liverpool going on to lift the 2019 Champions League after meeting the Parisians at the group stage – this is deliciously balanced. And extremely unlikely to end goalless. All together now: ♯♬ ♪ I love Paris in the springtime … why oh why do I love Paris? Because kick-off is near! ♬♩♬ That’s 8pm GMT to be exact. It’s on! Continue reading...
